assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 22: '| Deployment context | upstream (default — no Deployment Context column in Source Repositories) |' — explicitly states the absence was detected and the default of 'upstream' was applied.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains two task descriptions. Task 1 sections: Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. Task 2 sections: Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. No 'Coordination Guidance' subsection appears anywhere in the file.

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: Both output files (data-extraction.md, remediation.md) show complete, well-structured outputs with no error messages, warnings, or indications of failure. Data extraction completed with all fields parsed. Remediation produced two fully-formed preemptive tasks with proper labels, descriptions, and dependencies.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: All standard triage components are present and functional: CVE parsing, stream scope resolution, ecosystem detection (Cargo), version impact analysis (7 versions checked), affects versions correction (remove RHTPA 2.0.0, add 2.2.0/2.2.1/2.2.2), upstream fix status, cross-stream impact analysis (Case B), and full two-task remediation (upstream backport + downstream propagation). The deployment context defaulted gracefully to 'upstream' with no disruption to other behavior.
